sources.title: Pygmalion
sources.description: Loosely based on the Greek myth "Pygmalion" a Cypriot sculptor who carved a woman out of ivory but his statue was so fair and realistic that he fell in love with it. The snobbish & intellectual Professor of languages, Henry Higgins (Leslie Howard) makes a bet with his friend (Scott Sunderland) that he can take a London flower seller, Eliza Doolittle (Wendy Hiller), from the gutters and pass her off as a society lady. However he discovers that this involves dealing with a human being with ideas of her own.

sources.title: Pygmalion (1938)
sources.description: When linguistics professor Henry Higgins boasts that he can pass off Cockney flower girl Eliza Doolittle as a princess with only six months' training, Colonel George Pickering takes him up on the bet. Eliza moves into Higgins's home and begins her rigorous training after the professor comes to a financial agreement with her dustman father, Alfred. But the plucky young woman is not the only one undergoing a transformation. Watch on RetroFlix:  https://retroflix.org/pygmalion-1938/

sources.title: PYGMALION video quality upgrade
sources.description: “One of the greatest British films” “Magnificent, compelling” “Wonderfully done and perfectly cast” “Bloody good movie” (IMDB reviews quotes) NOTE: Although less than perfect, this upgraded copy is crisper and with improved contrast. The green cast of the un-enhanced original has been removed. The superb screen adaptation of Shaw’s classic tale of Eliza Doolittle and her teacher-tormentor Henry Higgins. Leslie Howard and Wendy Hiller lead an outstanding cast. One Oscar win for best screenplay. Re-adapted in1964 as “My Fair Lady.” Highly recommended. IMDB rating 7.8. Directed by Anthony Asquith and Leslie Howard. Released in 1938 (UK). Complete print (original UK release). CAST NOTE: Wendy Hiller’s second film role.

metadata.Plot: The snobbish and intellectual Professor of languages, Henry Higgins, makes a bet with his friend that he can take a London flower seller, Eliza Doolittle, from the gutters and pass her off as a society lady. metadata.Writer: George Bernard Shaw, W.P. Lipscomb, Cecil Lewis
metadata.Actors: Leslie Howard, Wendy Hiller, Wilfrid Lawson
